Project.name: @osovv/vv-opencode
Project.annotation: Portable OpenCode workflow package with plugins and a Bun CLI for install, sync, and cross-device setup.

Actors.actor-User: Engineer who wants a portable OpenCode workflow across devices and repositories.
Actors.actor-System: CLI and plugin runtime that install, sync, and execute the configured workflow safely.

UC-001.Action: Runs `vvoc install` for global or project scope.
UC-001.Goal: To register `@osovv/vv-opencode` in OpenCode config while keeping vvoc-managed settings in the canonical `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json` file.
UC-001.Preconditions: Bun and OpenCode are available, and the target config locations are writable.
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: The package name appears in the resolved OpenCode config and canonical `vvoc.json` is created under `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/`.

UC-002.Action: Runs `vvoc sync` on another machine or after package updates.
UC-002.Goal: To refresh canonical vvoc-owned config and managed prompt files without reintroducing scattered per-feature config files.
UC-002.Preconditions: The target OpenCode config and canonical vvoc config can be created or updated.
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: The canonical `vvoc.json` file is rewritten canonically and managed prompts are refreshed without requiring legacy config paths.

UC-003.Goal: To persist explicit durable context in the vvoc data root under `$XDG_DATA_HOME/vvoc/`, with `shared` scope visible across projects and local scopes isolated per project.
UC-003.Preconditions: The package is enabled in OpenCode and memory is not disabled in canonical `vvoc.json`.
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Session, branch, and project entries are project-local, shared entries are visible across projects, and stored memory is never silently injected into prompts.

UC-004.Goal: To audit stored memory and get keep/update/merge/delete suggestions without modifying entries.
UC-004.Preconditions: The package is enabled in OpenCode and explicit memory exists in scope.
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: The reviewer can only read memory tools and returns a report-only audit.

NonGoals.item-1: No automatic memory injection into prompts.
NonGoals.item-2: No vector store, embeddings, or indexed retrieval in v1 memory.
NonGoals.item-3: No fallback compatibility with old per-feature vvoc config files once canonical `vvoc.json` is introduced.

Constraints.constraint-1: OpenCode config integration must follow documented global and project path conventions.
Constraints.constraint-2: vvoc-managed settings must live in the canonical `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json` file.
Constraints.constraint-3: Persisted vvoc data must live under `$XDG_DATA_HOME/vvoc/`, not inside the OpenCode config tree.
Constraints.constraint-4: Config writes must be idempotent and preserve existing JSONC comments in the main OpenCode config where practical.
Constraints.constraint-5: Memory must stay explicit-only. Stored entries must not be injected into prompts automatically.
Constraints.constraint-6: Generated canonical vvoc config must be fully seeded and versioned by schema rather than split across multiple CLI-managed files.

Risks.risk-1: Repositories or machines may contain stale legacy vvoc config files that must be ignored once canonical `vvoc.json` is introduced.
Risks.risk-2: Unsafe rewrites of user-maintained config could damage trust in the CLI.
Risks.risk-3: The file-based memory store performs full scans and may degrade as the number of entries grows.
Risks.risk-4: Guardian and Memory depend on OpenCode plugin and SDK surface area that may evolve over time.

OpenQuestions.question-1: Should future versions also manage OpenCode skills and agents as installable assets in addition to plugin config and vvoc-owned files?
OpenQuestions.question-2: Should memory eventually grow an index or vector-backed search layer once the XDG data-backed store becomes large?
